basics.name: Wouter Nuijten
basics.summary: Final-year PhD candidate in Bayesian Machine Learning at Eindhoven University of Technology and Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Lazy Dynamics. I work on Active Inference as variational inference and build the open-source tooling (RxInfer.jl ecosystem) to make it run in real time.

courses: Research on Active Inference as variational inference: scalable, message passing-based planning and decision-making
Core developer of RxInfer.jl; author of GraphPPL.jl and RxEnvironments.jl

education.department: Department of Mathematics and Computer Science
courses: Thesis on Graph Neural Networks in particle shower reconstruction

education.institution: Eindhoven University of Technology & Tilburg University
education.department: Department of Mathematics and Computer Science, Tilburg Law School
courses: Minor in Business Analytics and Operations Research

work.name: Lazy Dynamics
highlights: Bringing the RxInfer.jl ecosystem to production in industrial applications
Built Gears.jl, a Julia package for fine-grained job scheduling in simulations and real-time systems
Fast-iterating, product-oriented work in a pre-seed startup environment

work.name: MakesPerfect
highlights: Founder of a Data Science Sport company
Supported KNVB in preparation & match analysis during FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022

work.name: Eindhoven University of Technology
highlights: Assisting Full Professors in their teaching of several courses

volunteer.summary: E.S.Z.V.V. Totelos is the student Futsal association of Eindhoven. With more than 200 members it is one of the largest student associations in Eindhoven.
highlights: Ensuring the financial stability of the association
Assisting the treasurer in the compilation of the yearly financial documents
Checking and approving financial plans proposed by the board
Writing an association policy and vision, ensuring the growth of the association through a multi-year policy
Incorporating association policy in financial plans and management

volunteer.summary: D.S.A. Pattern is the study association for the Data Science study programme, and is the starting point for the professional career of Data Science students.
highlights: Chairing a committee responsible for the external relations of the association
Creating and maintaining close relations with business partners
Executing the career-oriented vision of the university and the association
